title: Fouling abatement for coker heaters
abstract: A polymeric quaternary ammonium based antifoulant composition having a polyamine crosslinked reaction product of an alkylamine and epichlorohydrin; and at least one aliphatic alkylene diamine. A method of inhibiting fouling on structural parts of a system exposed to a hydrocarbon, the method providing adding an antifoulant composition to a hydrocarbon medium, wherein the antifoulant composition comprises a polyamine reaction product of an alkylamine and epichlorohydrin, and at least one aliphatic alkylene diamine.
claims: Claims 1. A polymeric quaternary ammonium based antifoulant composition, the composition comprising: a polyamine comprising the crosslinked reaction product of an alkylamine and epichlorohydrin; and at least one aliphatic alkylene diamine. 2. The composition as recited in claim 1, wherein the polyamine comprises the formula wherein R and R' is -CH 3 or -CH 2 CH 3 ; n ranges from about 10 to about 10,000; and wherein X is SCL 2- , Cl or C2H3O2 " . 3. The composition as recited in claim 2, wherein the polyamine has a molecular weight of about 1,000 to about 1,000,000. 4. The composition as recited in claim 2, wherein the polyamine is crosslinked with the at least one aliphatic alkylene diamine. 5. The composition as recited in claim 2, wherein the polyamine has a viscosity of about 5 cPs to about 1000 cPs. 6. The composition as recited in claim 1, wherein the polyamine comprises the formula wherein R and R' is -CH 3 or -CH 2 CH 3 ; and X is SO 4 2 , Cl or C 2 H 3 O 2 ; and n ranges from about 10 to about 10,000. 7. The composition as recited in claim 6, wherein the polyamine has a molecular weight of about 50,000 to about 7,000,000. 8. The composition as recited in claim 6, wherein the polyamine has a viscosity of about 100 cPs to about 7000 cPs. 9. The composition as recited in claim 6, wherein the polyamine is crosslinked with the at least one aliphatic alkylene diamine. 10. The composition as recited in claim 1, the antifoulant composition further comprises a dispersant. 11. The composition as recited in claim 10, wherein the dispersant is selected from formaldehyde resins, block copolymers, oligomeric surfactants, polyalkylene succinimides, and/or combinations thereof. 12. The composition as recited in claim 1, wherein the at least one aliphatic alkylene diamine is selected from ethylene diamine, propylene diamine, butylene diamine, dimethylaminopropylamine, and/or combinations thereof. 13. An antifoulant composition, the composition comprising: a reaction product of dimethylamine and epichlorohydrin; and at least one aliphatic alkylene diamine. 14. The composition as recited in claim 13, wherein the reaction product of dimethylamine and epichlorohydrin is crosslinked with the at least one aliphatic alkylene diamine. 15. A method of inhibiting fouling on structural parts of a system exposed to a hydrocarbon, the method comprising: adding an antifoulant composition to a hydrocarbon medium, wherein the antifoulant composition comprises a polyamine reaction product of an alkylamine and epichlorohydrin, and at least one aliphatic alkylene diamine. 16. The method as recited in claim 15, wherein the polyamine comprises the formula wherein R and R' is -CH 3 or -CH 2 CH 3 ; n ranges from about 10 to about 10,000; and wherein X is SCL 2- , Cl or C2H3O2 " . 17. The method as recited in claim 15, wherein the polyamine comprises the formula wherein R and R' is -CH 3 or -CH 2 CH 3 ; and X is SO 4 2 , Cl or C 2 H 3 O 2 ; and n ranges from about 10 to about 10,000. 18. The method as recited in claim 15, wherein the hydrocarbon medium is selected from a coker feed, liquid hydrocarbon feed, hydrocarbon stream, and/or vacuum residue. 19. The method as recited in claim 15, wherein the antifoulant composition is added to the hydrocarbon medium in an amount ranging from about 1 to about 1000 ppm by volume of the hydrocarbon medium. 20. The method as recited in claim 15, wherein at least one structural part is selected from coking units, delayed coking units, coker preheaters, cokers, coker furnaces, Visbreaker unit charge heaters, and/or related heat exchangers. 21. The method as recited in claim 15, wherein the antifoulant composition disperses deposits or particles within the hydrocarbon medium. 22. The method as recited in claim 15, wherein the antifoulant composition prevents the formation of deposits or particles present in the hydrocarbon medium. 23. A method for inhibiting the formation and deposition of coke on surfaces in contact with a hydrocarbon, the method comprising: adding a combination of a quatemized polyamine and a dispersant to a hydrocarbon, wherein said hydrocarbon is a hydrocarbon that is undergoing high temperature processing. 24. The method as recited in claim 23, wherein said hydrocarbon is a fluid hydrocarbon.
